heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Alexander Mackendrick. Written by Clifford Odets, Ernest Lehman and Alexander Mackendrick based on the novelette by Lehman.
clean: Directed by Alexander Mackendrick. Written by Clifford Odets, Ernest Lehman and Alexander Mackendrick based on the novelette by Lehman.

heading: J.J. Hunsecker
raw: Son, I don't relish shooting a mosquito with an elephant gun, so why don't you just shuffle along?
clean: Son, I don't relish shooting a mosquito with an elephant gun, so why don't you just shuffle along?

heading: J.J. Hunsecker
raw: I'd hate to take a bite outta you. You're a cookie full of arsenic.
clean: I'd hate to take a bite outta you. You're a cookie full of arsenic.

heading: J.J. Hunsecker
raw: Don't remove the gangplank, Sidney - you may wanna get back on board.
clean: Don't remove the gangplank, Sidney - you may wanna get back on board.

heading: J.J. Hunsecker
raw: You sound happy, Sidney. Why should you be happy when I'm not?
clean: You sound happy, Sidney. Why should you be happy when I'm not?

heading: J.J. Hunsecker
raw: Sidney, this syrup you're giving out with... you pour over waffles, not J. J. Hunsecker.
clean: Sidney, this syrup you're giving out with... you pour over waffles, not J. J. Hunsecker.

heading: Susan Hunsecker
raw: I'd rather be dead than living with you. For all the things you've done, J.J., I know I should hate you. But I don't. I pity you.
clean: I'd rather be dead than living with you. For all the things you've done, J.J., I know I should hate you. But I don't. I pity you.

heading: Steve Dallas
raw: The next time you want information, don't scratch for it like a dog, ask for it like a man!
clean: The next time you want information, don't scratch for it like a dog, ask for it like a man!

heading: Steve Dallas
raw: Tell me, sir: when he dies, do you think he'll go to the dog-and-cat heaven?
clean: Tell me, sir: when he dies, do you think he'll go to the dog-and-cat heaven?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sally: But Sidney, you make a living. Where do you want to get?
clean: But Sidney, you make a living. Where do you want to get?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: He thinks J.J.'s some kind of a monster...
clean: He thinks J.J.'s some kind of a monster...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan Hunsecker: Don't you?
clean: Don't you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: Susie, J.J. happens to be one of my very best friends!
clean: Susie, J.J. happens to be one of my very best friends!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan Hunsecker: I know. But someday I'd like to look into your clever little mind and see what you really think of him.
clean: I know. But someday I'd like to look into your clever little mind and see what you really think of him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: Where do you come off, making a remark like that?
clean: Where do you come off, making a remark like that?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan Hunsecker: Who could love a man who makes you jump through burning hoops like a trained poodle?
clean: Who could love a man who makes you jump through burning hoops like a trained poodle?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: Sure, the columnists can't do without us, except our good and great friend J.J. forgets to mention that. You see, we furnish him with items.
clean: Sure, the columnists can't do without us, except our good and great friend J.J. forgets to mention that. You see, we furnish him with items.

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: What, some cheap, gruesome gags?
clean: What, some cheap, gruesome gags?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: You print 'em, don't ya?
clean: You print 'em, don't ya?

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: What's this boy got that Susie likes?
clean: What's this boy got that Susie likes?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: Integrity - acute, like indigestion.
clean: Integrity - acute, like indigestion.

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: What does that mean - integrity?
clean: What does that mean - integrity?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: A pocket fulla firecrackers - looking for a match! [grinning] It's a new wrinkle, to tell the truth... I never thought I'd make a killing on some guy's "integrity."
clean: A pocket fulla firecrackers - looking for a match! It's a new wrinkle, to tell the truth... I never thought I'd make a killing on some guy's "integrity."

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: Match me, Sidney.
clean: Match me, Sidney.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sidney Falco: Not right this minute, J.J.
clean: Not right this minute, J.J.

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: Look, Manny, you rode in here on the Senator's shirt tails, so shut your mouth!
clean: Look, Manny, you rode in here on the Senator's shirt tails, so shut your mouth!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sen. Harvey Walker: Now, come, J.J., that's a little too harsh. Anyone seems fair game for you tonight.
clean: Now, come, J.J., that's a little too harsh. Anyone seems fair game for you tonight.

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: This man is not for you, Harvey, and you shouldn't be seen with him in public. Because that's another part of a press agent's life - he digs up scandal among prominent men and shovels it thin among columnists who give him space.
clean: This man is not for you, Harvey, and you shouldn't be seen with him in public. Because that's another part of a press agent's life - he digs up scandal among prominent men and shovels it thin among columnists who give him space.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sen. Harvey Walker: There is some allusion here that escapes me...
clean: There is some allusion here that escapes me...

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: We're friends, Harvey - we go as far back as when you were a fresh kid Congressman, don't we?
clean: We're friends, Harvey - we go as far back as when you were a fresh kid Congressman, don't we?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sen. Harvey Walker: Why does everything you say sound like a threat?
clean: Why does everything you say sound like a threat?

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: Manny, what exactly are the UNSEEN gifts of this lovely young thing that you manage?
clean: Manny, what exactly are the UNSEEN gifts of this lovely young thing that you manage?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Manny Davis: Well, she sings a little... you know, sings...
clean: Well, she sings a little... you know, sings...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda James: Manny's faith in me is simply awe-inspiring, Mr. Hunsecker. Actually, I'm still studying, but...
clean: Manny's faith in me is simply awe-inspiring, Mr. Hunsecker. Actually, I'm still studying, but...

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: What subject?
clean: What subject?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda James: Singing, of course... straight concert and...
clean: Singing, of course... straight concert and...

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: [glance flicks between Linda and the Senator] Why "of course"? It might, for instance, be politics...
clean: Why "of course"? It might, for instance, be politics...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda James: Me? I mean "I"? Are you kidding, Mr. Hunsecker? With my Jersey City brains?
clean: Me? I mean "I"? Are you kidding, Mr. Hunsecker? With my Jersey City brains?

heading: Dialogue
raw: J.J. Hunsecker: The brains may be Jersey City, but the clothes are Traina-Norell.
clean: The brains may be Jersey City, but the clothes are Traina-Norell.
